title: Tragacantha geyeri
taxonomy: Tragacantha geyeri is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Fabaceae. It is a member of the order Fabales and the genus Tragacantha. The species is also known as the Geyer's hairgrass or Geyer's needle grass, and is native to the southwestern United States. Tragacantha geyeri is further classified into the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Astragaleae. Its classification is part of the broader taxonomic hierarchy of the plant kingdom.

intro: Tragacantha geyeri, also known as Geyer's milkvetch, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Tragacantha geyeri include isoflavones, such as genistein and daidzein, as well as saponins and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Tragacantha geyeri include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of menopause.
